title: Level 303

{{Underline|A0A0A0}}__NOTOC__{{VoidedTheme}} {{Class Custom Image|entity=Undocumented Entities|hexagon=0|class=0|safety=Safe|security=Stable|bg-image=bd711e945bdf5a9d1cb2ae10de90f3d1.jpg|text-color=gray}} '''Level 303''' is the 304th level of the Backrooms, and its structures show some breathtaking modernist architecture that is also widely recognized to be an “art piece” within the Backrooms. ==Description== '''Level 303''', also known as Fair-Faced Concrete, is a level that's broken up into many different area sectors for unknown reasons. The appearance of these areas is somewhat similar to the Variations from [[Level 85|Level 85's]] structures. This area is wholly made of concrete structures, mostly walls. These walls and structures come without any coverage or paint on their surfaces. The structural layouts of Level 303 extend infinitely outward and never reach a limit, indicating that the level has no boundary. The borderlines of the various area sectors are connected and have soft transitions, which gives the feeling of walking through an art gallery exhibiting geometric complexes. The boundaries between indoor and outdoor structures are not strictly delineated but instead blurred to some extent: When walking into an area that resembles an entrance, a wanderer would often soon find themselves in another outdoor area. {{Blurimage|file=980f3091ee7dc85520a8386e89742d01.jpg|caption=The most iconic image of '''Level 303'''.}} Aside from bodies of concrete, 'Level 303’s' second most common feature is the exposed steel structures that are intertwined with wooden aisles and steps. Unlike most indoor spaces, which are usually cramped, this variant's defining characteristic is its unusual vastness and emptiness, with its aisles and steps covering areas wider than they appear. If one travels too far in any direction, they are likely to eventually encounter various types of water surfaces. The depth of these water surfaces is typically on the order of tens of centimeters, making them seem as if they barely rise above the ground and creating a visual effect similar to that of a lake or sea. Street-like structures and concrete bodies stretching along their edges are interspersed between the maze-like structures of other buildings. Beech trees are planted beside the roads, and those plants seem not to need any nutrition, growing in isolated and poor soil. There is typically nothing and no living matter on the roads, and the roads tend to extend for some distance, and then come to an abrupt end. Almost all buildings in '''Level 303''' appear to follow some pattern: Their heights and interior decorations are always limited to match the surrounding environment and highlight each other's appearance. This also makes the vast majority of building structures have their bases submerged up to ten meters below ground. Due to the visual perspectives, a lot of buildings in '''Level 303''' may be inaccessible due to numerous absurd obstacles formed by their own bodies and walls, or in some cases, their main structures may be hidden under the ground and hard to discover. This may mean that the actual structures at this level are more complex than most wanderers assume. The skies of '''Level 303''' are always in a gray/black tone, and the sources of all lights except for the lamps are unknown. The lights exist in all directions and always tend to create the most suitable angles for showcasing the shadows of the buildings and landscapes. The lamps in '''Level 303''' can still function, but neither the power sources nor a switch can be discovered. Despite their empty, run-down appearance, many buildings have not lost their functionality and purpose, such as residential buildings with appropriately placed furniture and underground shopping malls stocked with goods. In the different building areas, wanderers can experience different mind states and moods according to the atmosphere, and those atmospheres are created by the details of the areas themselves. In addition, directional signs still seldom appear in '''Level 303''', but they always hold no comprehensible texts. ===Spatial Variations=== The variations of '''Level 303'''’s spaces can be entered by a variety of ways; the most common way is to wander far enough into one direction or noclipping through a wall. These variations are no different from the regular areas, except for their special structures, each giving off its own unique characteristics.
  • Area 1
  • Area 2
  • Area 3
===Area 1: ”Lunar base”=== {{Underline|A0A0A0}} {{Blurimage|file=d402f7e0b145eb43c67db04cf4025feb.jpg|caption=A photo of the '''"Lunar Base".'''}} The “Lunar base” is the first space variation that has been discovered in '''Level 303'''. The majority of the Lunar base is composed of separate outdoor spaces with slate tiles laid on the ground. The daylight orientation never applies to the Lunar base, and the sky is always at its darkest. However, the structures and environments within this area, alongside the spaces themselves, have been shown to be fully visible even from outside in darkness. The fundamental cause behind this phenomenon is unknown. *The corridors and stairs found in the “Lunar base” appear to be narrower compared to those in the regular sections of this level, the height differences between the upper- and lower-layer platforms are always larger, and these various structures are all connected and interlinked by multiple non-linear and non-Euclidean spaces. Guardrails or fences do not exist on the Lunar base; therefore, wanderers may fall and get injured here. Apart from the overall structural differences, almost the entirety of the Lunar base is covered by a thin layer of dust, which is barely visible as it camouflages the gray concrete structures. Randomly discarded chairs and items can be found scattered throughout the Lunar base, indicating that the entire area seems to have been abandoned for a long time. Also, the temperature here tends to be lower than in other areas, giving off an overall desolate atmosphere. Many areas of the “Lunar Base” are inaccessible due to their non-Euclidean and complex structures, which sometimes keep them out of wanderers' reach. Also, the worrying lighting conditions can be a contributor the difficulty of travel. The indoor lights glowing between the concrete are generally round fluorescent lamps, but most of them are damaged due to disrepair. There is no evidence showing that the “Lunar Base” is physically connected to any other areas and sections, which could mean that this area is completely isolated from the rest of this level. The most common way to access this area is through an old iron door on Level 613. After entering, the space behind the door will disappear rapidly, and wanderers will find themselves inside the Lunar base. No edible supplies can be found in the “Lunar Base”, which also denies the possibilities of settling here. When wanderers walk off in this area for a certain distance far enough, the old door that was previously an entrance may reappear, and by entering this door, wanderers can be brought back to the regular areas of Level 303.
===Area 2: "Corridor"=== {{Underline|A0A0A0}} {{Blurimage|file=743797123adef35b2830078b7a3fb4a2.jpg|caption=A photo of the '''"Corridor"'''.}} The “Corridor” is a vertical body that often serves as a transition connecting different locations or spatial variants to the regular areas. It appears to be as a maze-like extended linear corridor, and the length of the intricate route usually fluctuates from hundreds of meters to tens of kilometers. No other objects or entities exist within the "corridor". All illumination in the area comes from the open space above the corridor, and even so, the temperature in the area is always below 20 degrees Celsius. Since this area is a transitional environment, the style and appearance of its interior may be influenced by that of the buildings near its entrance, but in most cases, the "Corridor" only appears to be slightly tilted inwards, which is believed to highlight the pressure and tension existing within the area itself. Contrary to popular belief, the Corridor does not extend in a simple straight line but rather intersects with other “Corridor” variants in certain areas. At the same time, the majority of the “Corridor” is located underground, as the “Corridor” itself tends to slope downwards at an imperceptible angle until it finally changes direction at hundreds of kilometers distanced from the entrance. The "Corridor" can exist in any area, most often at the corners of a building, in which there would exist a corridor that slowly leads downwards. If you noclip into a place that is alike to or resembles a "Corridor", you will have a certain chance of reaching the area. Due to the lack of supplies, long-term settlement there is considered unfeasible.
===Area 3: "Subterranean"=== {{Underline|A0A0A0}} {{Blurimage|file=WechatIMG77.jpg|caption=One of the exits of the "Subterranean".}} The “Subterranean” spatial variation is also the rarest variation of '''Level 303''' and was even once considered to be a sub-level of '''Level 303'''. This area appears as a large subterranean space structured mostly underground. In addition to concrete bodies, the grounds in this area are paved with large amounts of stone slabs, sand, and gravel. The illumination of "Subterranean" is primarily made up of natural sunlight coming from the round openings, which also makes it impossible to see anything in this area at night. The objects found in the "Subterranean" area are all in new condition, including, but not limited to, the wooden seats placed within the corridors and hallways and the glass panes embedded in the railings. Paintings and sculptures are placed and scattered throughout the "Subterranean" area, and small amounts of vegetation are planted in the rather open, sunny areas to integrate the environment with the interiors. There are no entities existing in this area, and bottles of almond water may appear in the corners of walls or on some chairs. Various tests show that the almond waters are totally drinkable, which also provides a guarantee for long-term residence in this area. However, the overly large area and complex structures make it extremely difficult for wanderers to hit up in this area. The "Subterranean" spatial variant can be accessed by noclipping in the "Corridor" or by noclipping to the ground. As the time spent wandering around increases, more exits will appear leading to the ground level. After reaching the exit, noclipping to the ground will bring wanderers back to the regular areas.
==Entities== No known entities have been found in '''Level 303''', and this is generally believed to be due to the vastness of the space and the complexity of the structures. ==Colonies and Outposts== For the reasons above, no bases or outposts can be established at this level. ==Entrances & Exits== ===Entrances=== When wandering through [[Level 0]], wanderers have a chance of encountering a concrete wall that differs from the yellow walls around it. Noclipping through that wall will bring wanderers to '''Level 303'''. ===Exits=== Theories of exits in [[Level 613]] vary, however, none of them have been proven to be true. {{Collapse|initial='''> Open Author & Licensing Information '''|final='''> Close'''|body= '''Author：''' [[User:ChizuruOfficial|千鹤-1]]
